outcomes.description: Read-only access to browse all items from the database and review the full audit log trail. Viewer cannot add items, change completion status, or delete items.

scenarios.scenario: Browse items and verify gateway connectivity
scenarios.description: Viewer arrives at the Item Management page. The app simultaneously checks gateway connectivity and loads all items from the database. The Viewer observes each item's name, description, and completion status. An empty state message is shown when no items exist.

actions.description: Sends a GET request to the gateway root endpoint with the X-Persona: Viewer header. The returned message populates the Gateway Status display. On connection failure the status shows 'Error connecting to gateway'.
Sends a GET request to the gateway items endpoint with the X-Persona: Viewer header. Returns the full list of items stored in the database. On error, logs to the browser console.

apis.response: Array of item objects: [ { id, name, description, completed } ]

actions.description: label: Gateway Status; source: status state variable; default: 'Loading...'; on success: the message string returned by the gateway; on error: 'Error connecting to gateway'; formatting: plain text displayed in the Gateway Status card heading

steps.step: Observe each item in the list

actions.description: label: Name; source: item.name; formatting: displayed in bold; emptyState: list shows 'No items found.' when no items exist
label: Description; source: item.description; formatting: plain text displayed after a colon following the item name; emptyState: list shows 'No items found.' when no items exist

label: Completion Status; source: item.completed (boolean); formatting: CSS class 'completed' applied to the list row when true, no class when false (item appears as pending); visibleTo: all personas — status is reflected via row styling; the status-change control is hidden for Viewer (skipped)

scenarios.description: Viewer reads the audit log entries loaded from the audit service via the gateway. Each entry shows when an action occurred, what operation was performed, which database table was affected, and the record identifier. An empty state is shown when no logs exist.

steps.step: Load audit log data from the gateway

actions.description: Sends a GET request to the gateway audit endpoint with the X-Persona: Viewer header. Returns all audit log records from the audit service. On error, logs to the browser console.

apis.response: Array of audit log objects: [ { id, created_at, action, table_name, record_id } ]

actions.description: label: Timestamp; source: log.created_at; formatting: new Date(log.created_at).toLocaleString() — converts ISO timestamp to locale-specific date and time string; displayed in square brackets at the start of each entry
label: Action; source: log.action; formatting: plain text string displayed inline after the timestamp; describes the operation recorded (e.g. INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
label: Table Name; source: log.table_name; formatting: displayed as 'on table {table_name}' after the action value; identifies which database table was affected
label: Record ID; source: log.record_id; formatting: displayed as '(Record ID: {record_id})' at the end of each log entry; identifies the specific database record that was affected

scenarios.scenario: Switch the active persona to control gateway request permissions
scenarios.description: Any user on the page selects a persona value from the persona selector. The selection updates the X-Persona request header used on all subsequent calls to the gateway and triggers an immediate reload of items and audit log data reflecting the new persona's access level.

actions.description: label: Persona; type: enum; required: true; default: Admin; options: Admin, User, Viewer; sets the X-Persona header value used on all subsequent API requests to the gateway; triggers re-fetch of items and audit logs via useEffect dependency on persona state

steps.step: Reload data with the updated persona header

actions.action: Retrieve the items list with the updated persona header
actions.description: Refetches all items from the gateway using the newly selected X-Persona header value. Gateway enforces RBAC rules for the new persona.

apis.response: Array of item objects: [ { id, name, description, completed } ]

actions.action: Retrieve the audit log entries with the updated persona header
actions.description: Refetches all audit log entries from the gateway using the newly selected X-Persona header value.

apis.response: Array of audit log objects: [ { id, created_at, action, table_name, record_id } ]

skippedComponents.reason: Pure styling, no interactive state or API calls
Entry point that renders App — no state or API calls
Pure global styling, no interactive state

skippedForVisibility.what: Add New Item form (name input, description input, Add submit control)
Complete/Undo status control per item row
Delete item control per item row
